explanation: This LTL property expresses that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state and both 'supported' (system health) and 'good' (sensor data) are true, then in the next step, the state shall be TRANSITION. This directly captures the requirement that a state change from PULLUP to TRANSITION must occur when the system is healthy and sensor data is good.

This property expresses that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state, and both the 'supported' signal is True (system is healthy) and sensor data is good, then in the next time step the system must transition to the TRANSITION state. We use the 'state_is_PULLUP' atomic proposition to detect when the autopilot is in PULLUP state, 'supported' for system health, 'good' for valid sensor data, and 'state_is_TRANSITION' for the required next state. The template 'G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3))' captures: 'if ((PULLUP) and (system supported) and (good sensor)) then (next state must be TRANSITION)'. N_DURATION is not needed for this property.

The property requires that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state, and both the system is supported and sensor data is good, then in the next step the autopilot must be in the TRANSITION state. We use 'state_is_PULLUP' for being in PULLUP, 'supported' (system healthy), 'good' (sensor data good), and 'state_is_TRANSITION' for the destination state. The formula ensures the transition occurs from PULLUP to TRANSITION when the required conditions are met. Template 41 expresses this as: always, if we are in PULLUP and the enabling conditions hold, then in the next time step, we move to TRANSITION.

The requirement states that the autopilot must transition from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state under the condition that both 'supported' and 'good' (sensor data is good) are true. This is a classic state change pattern: when the autopilot exits PULLUP (that is, was in PULLUP and next step is not in PULLUP), if 'supported & good' holds, then on the next step we should see the state become TRANSITION. The template that fits is one where when the system leaves a defined state (PULLUP), if 'supported & good' is true at this moment, on the next step the TRANSITION state must be active. This corresponds to template 60: ((G (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) -> (X bool_exp3))) & (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)). Here, 'bool_exp1' is 'state_is_PULLUP', and 'bool_exp3' is 'state_is_TRANSITION & supported & good'. This matches the necessary semantics: from any point in the system, whenever it leaves PULLUP, and at that point 'supported & good' is true, the next state should be TRANSITION (indicating the transition occurs if and only if both conditions are true).

To capture the system's requirement that the autopilot changes from PULLUP to TRANSITION only if 'supported' is true and sensor data is 'good', we must formalize a transition: when the system is in the PULLUP state and then, in the next instant, is in TRANSITION, this transition can only occur if 'supported' and 'good' are both true at the time of transition. The template 20 [ (G (((! bool_exp2) & (X bool_exp2)) -> (X bool_exp3))) & (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3) ] is suited for state change constraints: whenever 'bool_exp2' becomes true (state_is_TRANSITION) after being false (not in TRANSITION), then (on this edge) in the next state, 'bool_exp3' must hold (i.e., the system should be supported and good, and the PULLUP state was held just prior). We set bool_exp2 to 'state_is_TRANSITION' and bool_exp3 to 'supported & good & state_is_PULLUP'. This ensures that this transition may only happen if requirements are met, and also that if the system is in TRANSITION, then the constraints must hold (strengthening the property for completeness). This LTL formula captures the intent of the requirement.

The requirement states that the autopilot should transition from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state only when the system is supported and the sensor data is good. This is best interpreted in LTL as: whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state AND both 'supported' AND 'good' are true, then in the next step, the state becomes TRANSITION. Thus, we use the template 'G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3))' where bool_exp1 is 'state_is_PULLUP', bool_exp2 is 'supported & good', and bool_exp3 is 'state_is_TRANSITION'. This captures the required guarded state transition.

The requirement states that the autopilot should change from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state when two conditions are met: the system is supported and the sensor data is good. In terms of provided atomic propositions, 'state_is_PULLUP' and 'state_is_TRANSITION' identify the relevant states, 'supported' indicates general system health, and 'good' signals valid sensor data. To express "change states from PULLUP to TRANSITION when supported and good" in LTL, we want to capture that whenever the system is supported, sensors are good, and the system transitions from PULLUP to TRANSITION in the next state (i.e., is in PULLUP now and in TRANSITION in the next step), which can be formalized using a template that relates a conjunction of conditions to a next-state condition. Template 50 captures this: it asserts that whenever 'bool_exp1 & bool_exp2' (here: in PULLUP now, supported, and good) changes (i.e., becomes true in the next step after being false), then in the next step, the state is TRANSITION, and if (bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) holds now, then state_is_TRANSITION must also hold now - this best matches the transition semantics. Here, bool_exp1 = "state_is_PULLUP", bool_exp2 = "supported & good", and bool_exp3 = "state_is_TRANSITION". 'N_DURATION' is not necessary for this template.

The property specifies that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state, and both the system is supported and sensor data is good (i.e., 'supported' and 'good' are true), then in the next step the autopilot should be in the TRANSITION state. This reflects the natural language requirement that state should change from PULLUP to TRANSITION when both conditions (supported and good) are met. We use bool_exp1 = state_is_PULLUP, bool_exp2 = supported & good, and bool_exp3 = state_is_TRANSITION. Template 41 captures that, when both antecedents (PULLUP state and the conditions) are true, the system moves to TRANSITION in the next step.

This LTL property specifies that whenever the system is currently in the PULLUP state, and both 'supported' is True and 'good' is True (the system is healthy and sensor data is good), then on the next step, the autopilot shall enter the TRANSITION state. This precisely encodes the requirement that a transition from PULLUP to TRANSITION happens under the indicated conditions.

This LTL expresses that whenever the autopilot is currently in the PULLUP state and both 'supported' and 'good' are true (the system is healthy and sensor data is good), then in the next step the autopilot should be in the TRANSITION state. This directly captures 'shall change states from PULLUP to TRANSITION when the system is supported and sensor data is good.'
